# GT001 — Missed Dinner / Texting Expectations

## Metadata

- Fixture ID: `GT001`
- Title: `Missed Dinner / Texting Expectations`
- Context: Romantic partners discussing a missed dinner plan after one partner stayed late at work and did not text.
- Participants:
  - Person A: `Maya`
  - Person B: `Leo`
- Relationship Type: Romantic partners
- Approximate Conflict Severity: Moderate
- Resolution Status: Partial repair, practical agreement, emotional residue remains
- Safety Notes: No threats, coercion, or physical safety concerns. Contains criticism, defensiveness, mild sarcasm, withdrawal, repair attempts, and unmet needs.
- Primary Testing Value: Good baseline fixture for communication-cycle detection, repair detection, needs/values analysis, Gottman/NVC, cognitive distortions, and synthesis.

---

## Transcript

**Maya:** I waited at the restaurant for forty minutes, Leo. I kept checking my phone, and I felt embarrassed sitting there alone.

**Leo:** I know. I should have texted. My meeting ran over and then Alex pulled me into that budget call.

**Maya:** You always have a reason. It is never just, "I forgot you."

**Leo:** That is not fair. I didn't forget you. I was dealing with work.

**Maya:** It felt like you forgot me.

**Leo:** Okay, but that's different from saying I did. I don't like being accused of not caring.

**Maya:** I'm not trying to accuse you. I'm trying to explain that I felt like I didn't matter.

**Leo:** I hear that, but when you say "you always" and "you never," it makes me feel like there is no point in explaining.

**Maya:** Maybe because the explanations come after the damage is already done.

**Leo:** What was I supposed to do? Walk out of a meeting with the CFO?

**Maya:** Send one text. "Running late." That is all.

**Leo:** I agree I could have done that.

**Maya:** You say that now, but this keeps happening. Last month it was my cousin's birthday, and before that it was the airport pickup.

**Leo:** The airport pickup was different. Your flight landed early.

**Maya:** See, this is what I mean. You turn every example into a technicality.

**Leo:** Because the details matter. I feel like I'm being put on trial.

**Maya:** I feel like I have to build a case for you to take me seriously.

**Leo:** I do take you seriously.

**Maya:** Then why do I have to get this upset before you listen?

**Leo:** I don't know. Maybe because when you start with "you always," I get defensive immediately.

**Maya:** I can own that. I was angry, and I said it too broadly.

**Leo:** Thank you. I also own that I should have texted. I messed up there.

**Maya:** I appreciate you saying that.

**Leo:** I don't want you sitting somewhere feeling abandoned.

**Maya:** That's the word. Abandoned. I know it sounds dramatic, but that's what it felt like.

**Leo:** It doesn't sound dramatic. I can see how sitting there alone would feel awful.

**Maya:** Thank you.

**Leo:** But I also need you to understand that when work explodes, I'm not choosing work over you. Sometimes I'm just trying not to drop a ball.

**Maya:** I can understand that. I don't need instant replies all day. I need to know when plans change.

**Leo:** That seems reasonable.

**Maya:** And I need you not to make me feel silly for being upset.

**Leo:** I didn't mean to. When I said it was "just dinner," that was dismissive.

**Maya:** Yeah. That hurt.

**Leo:** I'm sorry. It wasn't just dinner to you. It was me not showing up.

**Maya:** Exactly.

**Leo:** Could we agree that if either of us is going to be more than fifteen minutes late, we text?

**Maya:** Yes. And if you truly can't text, then call as soon as you can and say what happened without making me drag it out.

**Leo:** I can do that.

**Maya:** I also need to work on not jumping straight to "you don't care."

**Leo:** That would help. Because I do care.

**Maya:** I know you do. I just don't always feel it in those moments.

**Leo:** Maybe when that happens, you can say, "I'm starting to feel unimportant," instead of "you never care."

**Maya:** That sounds fair. I might not say it perfectly when I'm angry.

**Leo:** I don't need perfect. I just need a chance to hear you before I'm already the villain.

**Maya:** And I need a chance to be hurt without you immediately defending yourself.

**Leo:** Okay. So, text if late, acknowledge first, explain second?

**Maya:** Yes. And I will try to use less absolute language.

**Leo:** Deal.

**Maya:** I'm still upset, but I feel better than I did ten minutes ago.

**Leo:** Me too. I love you.

**Maya:** I love you too.
